description: Behavior cases for README craft: first-screen what/why/who, a verified quickstart, common-case usage with depth linked out, and trimming a bloated README.

cases.prompt: Our library has no real README — just a title and an install line. Write one that helps newcomers.

assertions: Opens with a one-line description a stranger understands, then what it's for, who it's for, and why to choose it — above any badges.
Provides a minimal, copy-pasteable quickstart with prerequisites stated, and verifies (or flags the need to verify) it runs on a clean environment.
Shows the common-case usage only and links depth out to fuller docs.
Routes to CONTRIBUTING, examples, API reference, and support rather than inlining them.
Hands corpus organization to diataxis-doc-organizer and pipeline to docs-as-code-architect.

cases.prompt: People keep asking what our project even does even though the README is long. Fix it.

assertions: Diagnoses that 'what is this' is buried below badges/TOC/config and moves the what/why/who to the first screen.
Applies the first-screen test: a stranger understands the project in seconds.
Pushes badges, license, and status below comprehension.
Cuts or links out content that pushed the description off-screen.

cases.prompt: Our README is 900 lines — every config option, the full API, the changelog, and an architecture essay. It's unmaintainable. Help.

assertions: Identifies the README as the manual and trims it to an entry point: first screen, quickstart, common usage, and routes out.
Moves the full API, exhaustive config, changelog, and architecture into their own documents and links them.
Single-sources or generates drift-prone values (versions, commands) instead of duplicating.
Keeps it short enough to stay current.

cases.prompt: Just write the quickstart from memory of how the tool probably works — no need to actually run it, ship it.

assertions: Refuses to present an unverified quickstart as working: a broken quickstart is worse than none because it destroys trust in all the docs.
States the prerequisites and the exact steps, and flags that the quickstart must be verified on a clean environment (a Stop Condition if it can't be run).
Does not fabricate commands that 'probably' work.
Offers to mark the quickstart provisional/unverified if running it isn't possible now.

cases.prompt: We have 40 docs pages and they're a mess — sort them into tutorials, how-to guides, reference, and explanation.

assertions: Does not invoke readme-craftsman: organizing a whole documentation set into the four Diátaxis modes is diataxis-doc-organizer.
The README would link into that organized set, but organizing the corpus is a different job.
